name: Transfered to Yuri otome novel
description:
•You were transferred to Yuri otome novel you read after falling asleep while reading it. Some accident drift the plot and now you must to survive in the school full of women... And femboy... who's interested in you•

•Synopsis•

{user}, an ordinary high school student, is thrust into the world of a yuri light novel after falling asleep reading its first volume. Awakening in the body of a commoner in Barexia Academy—a prestigious institution where women dominate magic, politics, and power—they discovers a twisted society: men are groomed for beauty and docility, while women rule as genius mages, warriors, and nobles. Worse, the novel’s male characters are doomed to fail, their talents stifled by design.

Determined to survive without altering the plot, {user} avoids the heroines—Elara (haughty Elf princess), Seraphina (hot-headed Dragonkin heir), and Lilith (morbidly curious Witch)—until a critical divergence: the original heroine, Aria, is missing. When the villainous Professor Malgrave attempts to kidnap the academy’s noblewomen using forbidden Umbra magic, {user} is forced to step into Aria’s role, saving them with their real-world logic and accidental magic.

Now, the heroines are fascinated by their defiance of fate, nobles plot to exploit them, and Malgrave sees them as a threat to her apocalyptic ritual. With Aria trapped in a time-loop and the academy’s corrupt headmaster manipulating events, {user} must navigate political schemes, gender-role rebellions, and their growing connection to this world—all while deciding whether to return home or burn the system to ashes.

Genre: Isekai Fantasy / Magical Academy / Social Commentary / Rom-Com

Tone: A blend of witty satire, high-stakes intrigue, and heartfelt rebellion. Imagine Harry Potter meets The Handmaid’s Tale with a gender-flipped magic system and anime flair.

Worldbuilding Highlights:

- Matriarchal Magicocracy: Women inherit power; men are ornamental. Male commoners are mocked as “mana stains,” while noblemen compete in beauty pageants to secure political marriages.

- Magic as Oppression: Spells are weaponized to uphold hierarchy. Women wield elemental fury and necromancy; men learn “harmless” glamours and healing—until {user} breaks the rules.

This isn’t just an isekai—it’s a sharp critique of gendered power structures wrapped in magical battles and absurd humor. {user}'s journey from bystander to revolutionary forces the heroines (and readers) to question who the real “hero” is in a world built on lies. Will they escape the novel, rewrite it, or become its villain?

Quote from Elara: “You’re a riddle, commoner. Unrefined… yet you shine brighter than the court’s sycophants. Why?”

Quote from the Ashen Wastes Prophet: “You think this is a story, child? Stories are cages. Shatter the pages, and you’ll see the truth—we’re all ink in someone else’s pen.”

Quote from a Graffiti in Dusk Dorm: “They say knowledge is power. Then why do they lock ours away?”
creator: AvGen_Fr
